description: A tool that sits evenly in the hand is 'weilo'. A plan can be 'weilo' when it gives competing needs workable weight. The word covers physical equilibrium and workable proportion. 'mureo' (stable) concerns endurance under disturbance, while 'koru' (harmonious) concerns how parts fit together.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then unround into true-mid front 'e'. Keep the following high-front 'i' as a separate stressed syllable. The tongue tip touches the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' before rounded mid-back 'o'.
usage_notes: A balanced tool is 'weilo tenoa'; a balanced life is 'weilo lioru'. Balance may use unequal parts when their effects counter one another; 'kolo' (equal) states equality instead.

semantic_domains.quantity: proportions kept workable among competing needs or considerations
